# Transcrição: 02-Black and White in DC

- **Fonte:** 02-Black and White in DC
- **Duração:** 06:08
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:01]

Keep it going for Matthew Dix. Come on up Matthew. It's the summer of 1993. I'm 22 years old and the boss at my company asks me if I want to go abroad to start a new office. And without even asking a question I say yes, because anything is better than where I am right now. I'm living with a family of Jehovah Witnesses. They've converted their pantry off their kitchen into a bedroom and I'm sharing it with a guy named Rick who speaks in tongues in his sleep and their indoor pet goat. So wherever I'm going it's better than this. Except it's not. Because my manager's idea of abroad is Washington DC in the middle of the summer. And I've been to Washington in the summer and it is terrible. But then I see the list of the 12 people going to DC and Kim Mercier is on the list. And Kim's been working for the company for about two months and she is beautiful and funny. The company is essentially a bunch of idiots collecting unemployment and getting paid under the table. But Kim is a college student who has a brain and she's come to work with us for the summer and I'm in love with her. But she doesn't know I exist because I don't have any moves. The only move I have with girls is I stand as close as possible to them and hope they eventually notice me. Like proximity is my strategy. And Washington with Kim will increase my proximity. And so I'm excited. And so we arrive in DC and the townhouses that my boss described to us is a townhouse with two bedrooms and four beds and a pile of air mattresses in the middle of the living room. And after I lose a game of rock paper scissors I take an air mattress and go try to find a place in the townhouse to sleep. And I find a walk-in closet in between the two bedrooms. It's just big enough to fit my air mattress which is actually slightly bigger than the pantry I had been living in. So it's like a palace. And while I'm inflating my air mattress Kim peeks in and she sees that I have a private space. And she asks if she can share it with me. And I say yes because this is proximity. And then she says there's ground rules. She says I sleep in a bra and panties but we won't be having sex. And I say of course knowing I have two months of proximity to work on her. And I think it's going to be wonderful but it's terrible because if you are sleeping next to the girl you love and she's half naked and in your walk-in closet that is un-air conditioned.

## [03:01 - 06:03]

So she basically sweats on you all night long. And she drapes her arm over your chest and she'll drape her leg over your leg. It's brutal. For the entire month of July I don't sleep because I'm terrified that if I fall asleep before her it will be the night that proximity wins her over and she decides to have sex with me. But I will already be asleep and she'll roll over. So it is a terrible, terrible time for me. And then one night we go to the holiday end to the bar and I meet Karen. And Karen is the perfect girl for me because she talks to me first and she buys drinks for me and she asks me out and she's the bartender there. So after her shift I go out with Karen and I end up in her apartment. And I see Kim the next day at work and Kim says, you didn't come back to the closet last night. And I say, I know. I went out with Karen and she says, oh, and it's that oh that someone says who never liked you but should have liked you and now only likes you because someone else likes you. And so I'm a little upset. And so the next day is Saturday and I come back and I'm sitting on the couch and I'm watching the ball game and Kim comes and sits next to me and she says, what are you doing tonight? And I said, I'm going to go out with Karen again. She doesn't say anything for a little while. She watches the game with me. And then eventually she says, you should go out with me tonight. And part of me is honestly very excited because this is the girl I've been lusting over all summer. And I say, well, I'm going out with Karen tonight and I have a commitment to her and, you know, I'm thinking, you know, I'm kind of thinking how can I get out of my deal with Karen? And Kim says, but we're going to go back to Massachusetts in a couple weeks and Karen's going to be here and I'm going to be in Massachusetts. And, you know, now I'm really feeling good. And while I'm hemming and hawing, then Kim says, and Karen's black and I'm white. And I'm so angry because there's so many ugly, stupid things that Kim could have said that I would have overlooked. If she had said, like, I like to kick puppies, I would have kicked puppies with her. You know, it was 1993 or she said, I support Ross Perot. I would have been out picketing with her. If she had told me that, like, they use your illusion one in two albums of guns and roses were better than appetite for destruction, I would have said, like, fuck yes, they are. Anything she could have said but that. And so I got up and I left the living room and I went out with Karen, who broke up with me three days later. And so I was back in the closet with Kim three days later in her bra and panties and I laid down next to her and I did not wait to go to sleep that night.

## [06:03 - 06:08]

Thank you. Thank you.
